title: The Top 10 Herbal Remedies for Oily Hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas can be a great way to help manage oily hair. Oily hair can be a problem because it can make your scalp feel itchy and uncomfortable, and it can also cause you to lose hair over time. If left untreated, it can lead to a buildup of oil and dirt on the scalp, causing more problems in the future. Fixing oily hair is important because it can improve your self-confidence and make you feel more comfortable in your own skin.  Using herbal teas like Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis) can be beneficial for oily hair. Rosemary has natural antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to balance the oil production in the scalp, reducing the appearance of oily hair. It also improves circulation, which helps to promote healthy hair growth.  Another herb that can help is Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia). Tea Tree oil has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. It also helps to reduce inflammation, which can cause itchiness and discomfort.  To use herbal teas for oily hair, you can steep the herbs in boiling water, then strain the liquid and use it as a final rinse after shampooing. You can also add the herbs to your shampoo or conditioner for an extra boost.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a great choice for people with oily hair. This is because rosemary has natural properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The tea contains antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. Rosemary tea also has a natural ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, which can help to remove dirt and excess oil. Additionally, rosemary has been known to help to regulate the scalp's natural oil production, leading to a healthier and more balanced scalp. By drinking rosemary tea, you may notice that your oily hair becomes less oily and more manageable, and your scalp may feel healthier and cleaner.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for oily hair because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production and reduce the appearance of greasiness.

Carnosic acid is good for oily hair because it has antifungal properties that help control oil-producing scalp bacteria, reducing the amount of oil produced and leaving hair looking healthier and less greasy.
Caffeic acid helps control oily hair by reducing oil production and balancing scalp pH, which prevents excessive sebum buildup and leaves hair looking cleaner and healthier.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for oily hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with oily hair, start by boiling 1 liter of water and then adding 2-3 teaspoons of loose-leaf Camellia sinensis tea leaves. Allow the tea leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes to bring out their beneficial properties. Strain the tea leaves and let it cool down to room temperature. Once cooled, use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This will help to balance your scalp's natural pH and reduce oil production, leaving your hair looking healthier and less greasy. Alternatively, you can also add a few tablespoons of Camellia sinensis tea to your regular shampoo or conditioner to get the benefits of the tea while washing your hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil tea, is a natural remedy that can help with oily hair problems. The main ingredient, melaleuca oil, is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help control the growth of yeast and bacteria on the scalp. These tiny organisms can cause an oily scalp and lead to greasy hair. By using tea tree oil tea, the scalp is cleaned and balanced, reducing the production of oil. Additionally, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness. This can also help prevent the buildup of oil and dirt on the scalp, making it easier to maintain a healthy and balanced scalp. Regular use of melaleuca alternifolia tea can lead to a decrease in oil production and a reduction in greasy hair.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for oily hair because they have antifungal properties that help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and scalp irritation.
Quercetin helps to balance oil production in the scalp and reduce excessive sebum, resulting in healthier and more manageable oily hair.
Tannins are good for oily hair because they help balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and preventing grease buildup on the hair.
Polyphenols help control oily hair by reducing sebum production and balancing scalp pH, which in turn keeps hair looking cleaner and healthier for longer.

remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal teas for oily hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with oily hair, start by boiling one teaspoon of the dried leaves in two cups of water for five to seven minutes. You can also add a few dried leaves of other herbs like rosemary, sage, or peppermint to enhance the benefits. After boiling, let the tea cool down and then strain it to remove the leaves. Next, use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair, working from the roots to the ends, and leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing with cold water. Alternatively, you can use the tea as a hair mask by applying it to your hair and leaving it on for 15 to 20 minutes before shampooing. Repeat this process once or twice a week to help balance the oil production in your scalp and reduce greasiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, is a natural herb that can help with oily hair. When made into a tea, thyme's active ingredients can be applied directly to the scalp. The antibacterial properties in thyme help control the growth of bacteria that can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, thyme tea has antifungal properties, which can prevent the buildup of yeast on the scalp, another common cause of oiliness. When the scalp is balanced, it can produce less oil, resulting in healthier-looking hair. Thyme tea can also help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can lead to an overproduction of oil. By using thyme tea as a scalp treatment, people with oily hair may find that it helps to regulate their scalp's oil production, leaving their hair looking cleaner and healthier. Thyme tea can be applied to the hair and left on for a few minutes before rinsing, or used as a final rinse after shampooing to help lock in benefits.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for oily hair because it helps to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce oil production, making it easier to manage and keep hair looking healthy and clean.
Rosmarinic acid is good for oily hair because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, which can cause excess oil production.
Tannins are good for oily hair because they help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reduce sebum levels, and prevent excessive greasiness.
Polyphenols help to balance the oil production in the hair by reducing inflammation and antioxidant activity, which in turn can help to regulate the scalp's oil glands and prevent excessive oiliness.

I couldn't find any information on Camellia sinensis containing the bioactive compound Carvacrol. However, I can provide you with information on Carvacrol found in other plants.  Carvacrol doesn't seem to exist. However, Carvacrol in Oregano, a different plant, has been found to be beneficial for oily hair because it helps to regulate the balance of oil production in the scalp and reduce inflammation.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for oily hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal teas that help with oily hair, you'll need some dried green tea leaves. First, boil water and then add one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves to the water. Let the leaves steep for 3-5 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup. You can drink this tea as is, or add a slice of lemon or honey to taste. To use the tea for hair, you'll need to cool the liquid completely, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing. After washing your hair with shampoo, pour the cooled green tea over your hair, making sure to cover all of your hair evenly. Leave the tea on for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water. This process can help balance the oil production in your scalp, reducing the greasiness of your hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea is a great natural remedy for people with oily hair. The tea is made from the leaves of the lavender plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. When used as a hair rinse, the tea helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, which can become imbalanced and lead to excess oil production. The antioxidants in the tea also help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial properties help to control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness. By using Lavandula angustifolia tea as a hair rinse, people with oily hair can experience a reduction in oil production and a healthier, more balanced scalp. The tea can be used as a rinse after shampooing, or as a final rinse to help close the hair cuticle and lock in moisture.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for oily hair because it helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production and reduce excess sebum, thus controlling oiliness and promoting a healthier scalp.
Linalyl acetate is good for oily hair because it helps to balance oil production in the scalp, reduces sebum levels, and leaves hair looking clean and healthy.
Rosmarinic acid helps to control oily hair by reducing sebum production and balancing the scalp's natural pH, leading to a healthier and less greasy hair appearance.
Apigenin is good for oily hair because it helps to reduce oil production in the scalp and also has antioxidant properties that prevent damage to the hair and scalp.
Tannins are good for oily hair because they help to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce excess oil production by closing pores, which in turn minimizes the greasiness and adds shine to hair.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for oily hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Camellia sinensis herbal tea that helps with oily hair, you need green tea leaves. Start by boiling fresh water in a kettle or pot. Then, add 1 to 2 teaspoons of green tea leaves to a cup or teapot. Let the leaves steep in the boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes. Strain the tea leaves from the liquid with a spoon or a tea strainer, and discard the leaves. Next, let the tea cool down to room temperature or slightly warm it up. After shampooing your hair, apply the cooled green tea to the scalp and massage it gently. Leave the tea on your scalp for 15 to 20 minutes before rinsing it off with warm water. This process can help balance the oil production in your scalp, reducing oily hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is a great way to help with oily hair. The tea's main ingredient, lemongrass oil, has natural astringent properties that help balance the scalp's oil production. When you drink this tea, it helps to reduce the amount of oil your scalp produces, which means less oil ends up on your hair. This can be especially helpful for people who have oily hair and find it difficult to keep it looking healthy and clean. The tea also has antifungal properties that can help control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, lemongrass oil has been shown to help stimulate blood flow to the scalp, which can help to unclog pores and reduce oil buildup. By drinking Cymbopogon citratus tea regularly, you can help keep your hair looking healthy and less greasy.

Linalool helps to control oily hair by balancing the scalp's natural oil production and reducing inflammation that can lead to excess oil and greasiness.

Polyphenols help control oily hair by reducing inflammation in the scalp and balancing the production of sebum, the natural oil that can cause greasiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for oily hair because it helps control oil production. The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, which can cause more oil to be produced. It also has antifungal properties that prevent yeast growth on the scalp, which can lead to oily hair and dandruff. The tea's astringent properties help dry out the scalp, balancing the oil production and reducing greasiness. Aloe barbadensis tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals that promote healthy hair growth, making it a beneficial treatment for oily hair. When applied to the hair, the tea's soothing properties calm the scalp and reduce oiliness, leaving the hair looking healthier and shinier.

After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer to remove the leaves. This will help balance the scalp's pH levels and reduce oil production, leaving your hair looking clean and fresh for a longer period.

The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness. Regular use of Saponaria officinalis tea as a hair rinse can lead to a reduction in oiliness, leaving hair feeling cleaner and looking healthier. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants can help to protect the hair and scalp from environmental stressors and damage.

Next, add two tablespoons of dried Camellia sinensis leaves, which come from the tea plant, to the boiling water. Allow the leaves to steep for 3-5 minutes, then strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large container. Discard the tea leaves and let the tea cool to room temperature. Once cooled, use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair, from the roots to the ends, and let it sit for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water. Repeat this process once or twice a week to help balance the natural oil production in your scalp and reduce oily hair.

This can lead to a reduction in oil production, as the scalp is less stressed and irritated. Additionally, lemon balm tea has antiseptic properties that can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. When consumed as a tea, lemon balm can also help to balance the body's hormones, which can influence oil production. Some people find that drinking lemon balm tea helps to reduce the amount of oil their hair produces, leading to a healthier and more manageable scalp. The tea can also be used as a hair rinse, where it can help to balance the pH of the scalp and reduce oil production. This makes it a popular natural remedy for people with oily hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea, made from the leaves of the sage plant, is a natural remedy for oily hair. It works by balancing the scalp's natural oil production. The tea's active ingredients help to reduce the amount of oil produced by the scalp's sebaceous glands. This results in less oil being released onto the hair, making it less greasy and heavy. The tea's antifungal properties also help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can weigh the hair down and make it look greasy. By using Salvia officinalis tea, people with oily hair can help to keep their locks clean, manageable, and looking healthy.

supplementary_best: To remedy oily hair, a combination of 'Melaleuca alternifolia', 'Thymus vulgaris', and 'Cymbopogon citratus' is recommended. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' (Tea Tree Oil) helps to reduce oil production and control scalp infections due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties. 'Thymus vulgaris' (Thyme) has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to balance scalp pH and reduce oiliness. 'Cymbopogon citratus' (Lemongrass) helps to improve scalp circulation, reducing oil buildup. This combination works well because 'Melaleuca alternifolia' addresses the root cause of oiliness (scalp infections), 'Thymus vulgaris' balances scalp pH, and 'Cymbopogon citratus' improves scalp circulation. This blend is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for daily use.
supplementary_avoid: If you have oily hair, some herbal teas can make it worse. Tea made from chamomile flowers (Matricaria chamomilla) should be avoided because it can stimulate oil production in the scalp. This can lead to even more oiliness and a greasier look.  Another herbal tea to avoid is ginger tea (Zingiber officinale). Ginger has warming properties that can increase blood flow to the scalp, causing more oil glands to produce sebum. This can exacerbate oily hair problems.  Eucalyptus tea (Eucalyptus globulus) is not suitable for people with oily hair either. Eucalyptus oil can help to dry out the scalp, but it can also strip the hair of its natural oils, leading to dryness and dandruff. However, if you have oily hair, you may be more likely to experience a greasy scalp after drinking eucalyptus tea.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice can help with oily hair because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce the production of oil in the scalp. When applied to the hair, it helps to balance the scalp's pH levels, which in turn reduces oil production. Apple cider vinegar is another natural remedy that helps with oily hair. It balances the scalp's pH levels and helps to close the cuticles of the hair, making it less likely to absorb excess oil. Baking soda is also effective in reducing oily hair by balancing the scalp's pH levels and absorbing excess oil. Egg whites can help to absorb excess oil and reduce the appearance of oily hair. When applied to the hair, egg whites help to tighten the cuticles of the hair, making it less likely to absorb excess oil.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with oily hair are often prone to other skin issues, such as acne and blackheads. This is because the same glands that produce excess oil in the scalp also produce it in other areas of the face and body. For example, the sebaceous glands in the nose and forehead can become clogged, leading to blackheads and acne.  The good news is that medicinal herbs like neem and tea tree oil can help to combat these issues. Neem, in particular, has natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the skin. When applied topically, neem can also help to unclog pores and reduce the appearance of acne and blackheads.  Tea tree oil, on the other hand, has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help to combat fungal infections like ringworm, which can often appear on the scalp and face of people with oily hair. It can also help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin.  Other herbs like aloe vera and chamomile can also help to calm and soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne and other skin issues. Aloe vera, in particular, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm and soothe irritated skin, while chamomile can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can sometimes contribute to skin issues.  Additionally, people with oily hair may also be prone to dandruff and itchy scalp. This is because the excess oil can attract dirt and other debris, leading to irritation and inflammation. Herbs like sage and rosemary can help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching. Sage, in particular, has natural antiseptic properties that can help to kill bacteria and fungi that can contribute to dandruff and other scalp issues.
